steps.title: Set up equations using the table data
steps.content:
The cost function has a fixed fee $F$ for the first hour and an hourly fee $H$ for each additional hour.

For $x$ hours: $f(x) = F + H(x - 1)$

From the table:
  • At 4 hours: cost = 90, so $F + 3H = 90$
  • At 6 hours: cost = 130, so $F + 5H = 130$

steps.title: Solve for the hourly fee, then the fixed fee
steps.content:
Subtract the first equation from the second to eliminate $F$:
$$[F + 5H] - [F + 3H] = 130 - 90$$
$$2H = 40$$
$$H = 20$$
Substitute $H = 20$ into the first equation:
$$F + 3(20) = 90$$
$$F = 30$$
Thus $H = 20$ (hourly fee) and $F = 30$ (fixed fee).

steps.title: Write the function and identify the answer
steps.content:
The cost function is:
$$f(x) = 30 + 20(x - 1)$$
$$f(x) = 30 + 20x - 20$$
$$f(x) = 20x + 10$$
That matches choice C — C is correct.
